items.title: Canada's economy lost 84,000 jobs in February, unemployment rate ticked up to 6.7%
items.summary_auto: Canada's economy lost 84,000 jobs in February, while the unemployment rate edged up to 6.7 per cent, Statistics Canada said on Friday.

items.title: U.S. moves to exempt Russian oil from sanctions for at least 30 days
items.summary_auto: The United States issued a 30-day licence for countries to buy Russian oil and petroleum products currently stranded ‌at sea in what Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said was a step to stabilize global energy markets roiled by the Iran war.

items.title: Canada's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scores 20 points for 127th straight game, breaking Wilt Chamberlain's NBA record
items.summary_auto: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander reacted to his latest individual accomplishment the way he always does: by deflecting credit and pointing to team goals. The Oklahoma City Thunder guard broke Wilt Chamberlain's record for consecutive games with 20 points or more, extending his streak to 127 on Thursday night.

items.title: Hospital ERs in Canada are at a breaking point and have been for some time. Is this the new normal?
North Bay, Ont., tenant says he’s worse off after accepting landlord’s $40K offer to move out
items.summary_auto: After a months-long fight with his landlord, Shannon Lucas accepted a $40,000 payout to move out of the North Bay, Ont., townhouse where he lived for eight years, but says he would give it all back if he could stay.

items.title: N.S. doctor's invention featured in an episode of The Pitt, HBO's hit medical drama
items.summary_auto: The Ring Rescue appeared in the latest episode of the HBO series after one of the device's inventors, Dr. Kevin Spencer, met the show's lead actor, Noah Wyle, and producers at a medical trade show last year.

items.title: Nova Scotia doctor's invention featured in hit medical drama The Pitt
items.summary_auto: The Ring Rescue appeared in the latest episode of the HBO series after one of the device's inventors, Dr. Kevin Spencer, met the show's lead actor, Noah Wyle, and producers at a medical trade show last year.

items.title: U.S. pressure on Venezuela hasn't eased, even as world's attention turns to Iran
items.summary_auto: Venezuela's acting president, Delcy Rodríguez, is trying to manage U.S. demands as she looks to hang on to the support of the Trump administration more than two months after it toppled Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro. It's a prospect that's being made more difficult because the U.S. is now at war with Iran.

items.title: Poilievre to focus on trade and auto sector as he launches U.S. trip in Detroit
items.summary_auto: Canada's Opposition leader is launching his trip to the U.S. with a stop in Detroit to talk about the automotive industry and cross-border trade, per a press release.

items.title: Canada's hospital emergency rooms have hit a breaking point. Is it the new normal?
items.summary_auto: Hospital emergency rooms are overflowing, resulting in patients being treated in storage rooms and hallways as they wait days for a bed. Doctors say it's happening against a backdrop of behind-the-scenes backlogs, access issues and an aging population that are all impacting the flow of patients through the health-care system.

items.title: Amid an energy crisis, the world is drawing on its oil reserves. Why doesn't Canada have any?
items.summary_auto: Because Canada is a net exporter of oil, it doesn't need to keep reserves. But some say that rule is outdated, and our at-capacity oil industry can't do much to help fill the gap created by stalled tanker traffic in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: How rare is it for a prime minister to attract 4 floor-crossers in 4 months?
items.summary_auto: Floor-crossing has always been a feature of Canadian politics dating back to the first Parliament — and seeing four opposition MPs jump ship to join the government in a matter of months is rare but not unprecedented.

items.title: 'Cascading effects' of Strait of Hormuz blockage getting worse by the day, energy and trade experts say
items.summary_auto: Rising oil prices are just the start. By blocking the Strait of Hormuz, global supply chains are at risk. For every day the war drags on, the list of product shortages grows. Shortages of fertilizer for farmers, critical minerals, and helium for semiconductor production are already being felt around the world.

items.title: Kash Patel touts 'tremendous opportunity' for FBI agents to train with UFC fighters
items.summary_auto: Interim lightweight champion Justin Gaethje will be among several UFC fighters who will demonstrate their training techniques to academy students and FBI staff at the FBI Special Agent Academy in Quantico, Virginia, on Saturday and Sunday.

items.title: Suspended doctor faces 28 new sex-crime charges, including sexual touching of minors
items.summary_auto: Suspended physician David Edward-Ooi Poon has been charged with 28 more sex crimes this week, for a total of 71 alleged offences. Toronto police now accuse the doctor and public health expert with three counts of sexual interference with minors and seven additional counts of sexual assault.

items.title: Alberta hospitals must admit patients to prevent ER backlogs, says review into Edmonton man's death
items.summary_auto: To help prevent a backlog of patients in emergency rooms, Alberta hospitals should not cap the number of patients who can be admitted to wards, says a review completed in the wake of a 44-year-old Edmonton man’s death in an ER.

items.title: Man found dead in boat off Belize coast identified as Canadian from Vancouver
items.summary_auto: Francis Usher, the CEO of the Ministry of National Defence in Belize, said police are treating the boat as a potential crime scene, because "apparent wounds" were found on the body, which was in a state of decomposition.

items.title: At Issue | Why are so many MPs crossing to Carney’s team?
items.summary_auto: At Issue this week: A fourth member of parliament ditches their party to join Mark Carney’s Liberals. The Conservatives push the government to step up deportations of Iranian Revolutionary Guard members. And Pierre Poilievre takes his speaking tour to the U.S.

items.title: As war between Israel and Hezbollah escalates, life in Lebanon poised to get 'uglier day by day'
items.summary_auto: Almost two weeks into the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran, the conflict in Lebanon is quickly escalating and expanding into more regions of the country. It began after the Iranian-backed militant group Hezbollah fired rockets into Israel, pulling Lebanon back into a major conflict with Israel.

items.title: Submarines off the table as Carney meets with leaders of Norway, Germany
Carney announces $35B for defence, infrastructure in Canada's North
items.summary_auto: The Liberal government has unveiled a detailed, multibillion-dollar proposal to modernize and expand Canada's military footprint in the country's Far North.

items.title: Winnipeg to host Juno awards in 2027
items.summary_auto: Many of Canada's brightest music stars will descend on Winnipeg for the Juno Awards in 2027.

items.title: How astronaut Jeremy Hansen's moon mission is inspiring students at his Ingersoll, Ont., alma mater
items.summary_auto: A lunchtime Lego project at Jeremy Hansen's former high school in Ingersoll, Ont., has grown into a mission to follow his upcoming Artemis II moon journey, and the Canadian astronaut's parents paid ode to the students' efforts by visiting their son's alma mater — with a special package.

items.title: KPop Demon Hunters sequel on the way, Netflix confirms
items.summary_auto: Netflix confirmed on Thursday that a sequel to KPop Demon Hunters, the streaming platform's most watched film of all time, is officially in development.

items.title: Silence from Ottawa 'shameful' after report of Canadian camp hit by missile: Conservative MP
items.summary_auto: Conservative defence critic James Bezan says it's "really shameful" the federal government did not tell the public that the airbase in Kuwait, where the Canadian Armed Forces have a camp, was hit by an Iranian missile attack on March 1.

items.title: B.C. chiefs tell MP Aaron Gunn to 'chillax' about land acknowledgments
items.summary_auto: The chiefs of four British Columbia First Nations have told Conservative member of Parliament Aaron Gunn to "chillax" after he criticized land acknowledgments spoken before public events.

items.title: Ryan Wedding co-accused linked to Toronto police corruption probe moved to special jail unit
items.summary_auto: An alleged drug smuggler for accused kingpin Ryan Wedding – recently linked to a man charged in a sprawling Toronto police corruption probe – has been moved to a special jail unit. Gurpreet Singh's lawyer told a court about the development, days after CBC News revealed Singh's ties to Project South.

items.title: Sinners or OBAA? Predicting the most unpredictable Oscars in years
items.summary_auto: The results of the 2026 Academy Awards feel harder to guess than any ceremony in living memory. To help you prepare for what is sure to be a nail-biting, shocker of a night, we've broken down the biggest categories — and the razor's-edge battles taking place in them.

items.title: This Yellowknife athlete just set 2 records by hopping 66 metres on his knuckles at the Arctic Winter Games
items.summary_auto: An athlete from the Northwest Territories has set two new records by hopping 66.142 metres on his knuckles at the Arctic Winter Games.

items.title: Israeli military drops charges against soldiers accused of sexual, physical abuse of Palestinian detainee
items.summary_auto: Israel's military on Thursday said it was dropping charges against five soldiers accused of sexually assaulting a Palestinian detainee in an incident partially caught on camera. The decision closed a case that has bitterly divided the country since the soldiers were arrested in 2024 at the notorious Sde Teiman military prison.

items.title: Part of Whistler ski resort closed following Thursday morning rockslide
items.summary_auto: Whistler Blackcomb owner Vail Resorts says the rockslide happened before the ski resort opened and that no one was injured.

items.title: Edmonton police chief faces backlash for 'educational' meeting with Israeli police
items.summary_auto: Edmonton’s police chief is facing criticism for travelling to Israel in February to meet with state policing officials as part of a counterterrorism delegation.

items.title: Former rapper's political party sweeps Nepal election
items.summary_auto: A political party created just four years ago and led by an ex-rapper has swept Nepal's parliamentary poll, results published by the electoral commission on Thursday showed.

items.title: A better way to measure individual climate action
items.summary_auto: In this issue of our environmental newsletter, we look at a new way to measure and manage your climate impact; find out why scientists are concerned about a plan to launch a million satellites; and check out how a B.C. First Nation is using solar energy, while encouraging renewables in other Indigenous communities.

items.title: FBI investigating Detroit-area synagogue attack as 'targeted act of violence against the Jewish community'
items.summary_auto: The armed man who rammed his vehicle into one of the largest Reform synagogues in the U.S. Thursday has been identified as a 41-year-old naturalized citizen born in Lebanon, according to federal officials.

items.title: Rice shipment heading for Toronto food banks struck near Iran, CEO says
items.summary_auto: Effects of the Mideast conflict are now reverberating in Toronto after a shipment of rice meant for the city's Daily Bread Food Bank was struck Wednesday in the Strait of Hormuz near Iran, the food bank's CEO says.
